expected_answer.root_cause: The JDBC Sink Connector processes large batches within a single database transaction in JdbcSinkTask.java. When batch.size is large, the JDBC transaction duration exceeds the PostgreSQL statement_timeout. The Kafka Connect framework's WorkerSinkTask.java offset flush timeout interacts with the JDBC transaction timeout, creating a race condition where the transaction is rolled back but offsets may still be committed.
error_chain: WorkerSinkTask.java polls a large batch of records from Kafka
WorkerSinkTask calls SinkTask.put() which delegates to JdbcSinkTask.put()
JdbcSinkTask opens a JDBC transaction and delegates to BufferedRecords for batch insert
BufferedRecords executes batch INSERT statements within the open transaction
PostgreSQL statement_timeout fires, rolling back the transaction
JdbcSinkTask receives SQLException and throws ConnectException
WorkerSinkTask catches the exception and marks the task as FAILED
Task enters restart loop, reprocessing the same large batch
affected_components: JDBC Sink Connector transaction management (JdbcSinkTask, BufferedRecords)
Any JDBC Sink Connector with large batch.size and database-side timeouts
expected_answer.remediation: Reduce batch.size to ensure JDBC transactions complete within the database statement_timeout. Alternatively, increase the database timeout or configure the JDBC connector to use sub-batching within transactions. Align offset.flush.timeout.ms with the expected maximum transaction duration.
